## Goal

Figure out why the implementation-rig reviewer engine almost never rejects work — is it because astrolabe specs are so tight that implementations genuinely have little to fail on, or is the reviewer applying too weak a standard? The answer determines whether we strengthen the reviewer, remove it, or rethink the artificer loop's shape. A reviewer that always passes is a rig stage burning cost and wall-clock for no signal.
